data.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent 72d5 (epic B: migrate plan-review + completion-verification onto the workflow engine) vs THIS child 92fa (B-UX: visual editor — select/create criteria, prompts & overlay triggers)]

4-bucket audit of the parent's criteria against THIS child, with whole-roster cross-check for any uncovered parent criterion:

Parent AC/SC → coverage by THIS child:
- AC1 (plan-review as engine workflow: overlay-resolved criteria → batch → verify → decide → coach; DET precheck; production batch-runner from sizing.py): UNCOVERED by this child (covered by sibling B2 6b06 + B1 723c).
- AC2 (verify_completion as engine workflow, uses→prompt→uses→uses): UNCOVERED by this child (covered by sibling B3 dc58).
- AC3 (planned-trace parity harness, offline corpus, CI): UNCOVERED by this child (covered by sibling B4 101a).
- AC4 (cutover bespoke→workflow; close/claim gates keep working; signing intact): UNCOVERED by this child (covered by siblings F5 f12d + 0408).
- AC5 (remove throwaway samples; shims preserved; epic A demo retained as visual-editing refinement sample): UNCOVERED by this child (covered by sibling B6 7e48).
- AC6 (end state: exactly two real workflows + one sample; lint + full suite pass): UNCOVERED by this child (covered by sibling B6 7e48).
- Success criterion (both gates on ONE engine substrate, prompts in library, parity-validated, samples removed): UNCOVERED by this child (composite of B1/B2/B3/B4/B6).

Cross-check result: every parent acceptance/success criterion IS covered by some OTHER sibling in the roster, so there is no "uncovered parent criterion" finding.

FINDING (structural bucket — non-traceable child):
- finding: THIS child (B-UX visual editor) does not contribute, fully OR partially, to ANY of the parent epic's acceptance or success criteria. The parent epic's scope is exclusively (a) migrating plan-review onto the engine, (b) migrating completion-verification onto the engine, (c) planned-trace parity validation, (d) cutover with signing intact, and (e) removing the throwaway sample workflows — none of which is editor UX. The parent's only mention of "visual editing" is AC5's retention of epic A's demo workflow "as the visual-editing refinement sample" (i.e. retaining a SAMPLE for the editor to refine against — covered by B6), not a commitment to deliver an editor upgrade. The parent "What"/"Out of scope" never names a visual editor, library pickers, or in-editor authoring. B-UX (and its backing data-model sibling B-DM 5506) therefore falls under this epic structurally without an anchoring parent criterion.
- criteria: [parent AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6, parent success criterion]
- location: child 92fa Acceptance Criteria (all five); parent 72d5 "What" / "Out of scope" / "Acceptance Criteria" / "Success Criteria"
- evidence: ["Parent SC: 'plan-review and completion-verification run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate ... validated by offline planned-trace parity ...; only real workflows remain (samples removed)' — no editor deliverable", "Parent AC5: retains epic A's demo workflow 'as the visual-editing refinement sample' — a sample to refine against, not an editor-upgrade deliverable", "Child 92fa ACs are entirely about editor dropdowns / inline trigger create / in-editor criterion+prompt authoring / Playwright E2E — none of which maps to any parent AC", "Whole-roster cross-check: parent AC1→B2(6b06)/B1(723c); AC2→B3(dc58); AC3→B4(101a); AC4→F5(f12d)+0408; AC5→B6(7e48); AC6→B6(7e48) — all parent criteria covered without B-UX"]
- scenarios: ["Epic 72d5 reaches its end-state (two real workflows + one sample, lint+suite green) with B2/B3/B4/F5/B6 done but B-UX still open → the epic's AC/SC are fully satisfiable yet a child remains, signalling B-UX is mis-parented or the epic is silently broadened beyond its stated scope.", "A reviewer tracing parent criteria → children finds B-UX (and B-DM) with no parent anchor, and cannot tell whether the epic's 'remove sample stubs / two-workflow end state' goal depends on the editor work or not."]
- impact: Traceability gap: a child carries effort (front-end rework, new editor-server endpoint, Playwright E2E, a backing B-DM data-model sibling) that no parent criterion accounts for, so the epic's acceptance set does not bound its actual deliverables. The epic can be "done" per its ACs while B-UX/B-DM are still open (or vice-versa), making completion ambiguous. No parent criterion is left uncovered, so this is a child-without-parent-anchor (scope-broadening) finding, not a coverage gap.
- checklist_item: The union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion — 4-bucket audit (fully/partially/uncovered/structural); an uncovered criterion is a finding.
- suggested_fix: Either (a) add a parent acceptance criterion that makes the editor/data-model work an explicit epic deliverable (e.g. "the visual editor lets a human select/author the criteria+prompts of the migrated plan-review batch, backed by the library write/enumerate data model"), tying B-UX + B-DM to the epic's intent; or (b) if the editor work is genuinely a separate concern, re-parent B-UX (and B-DM 5506) out of this epic so 72d5's children are exactly the migration/parity/cleanup stories its ACs enumerate. The choice should also note whether the epic's "done" (AC6 end-state) is gated on the editor work or independent of it.
